title: Set 01 - ML Basics, Regression, Validation, Clustering, Trees & Neural Networks
subject: ML
topic: ML Basics, Regression, Validation, Clustering, Trees & Neural Networks

questions.question: Point out the wrong statement:
options: ML algorithms do not understand context.
Machine learning is programming computers to optimize a performance criterion using example data or past experience.
With unsupervised learning, it is easy to handle real-world scenarios.
questions.description: Correct answer: C. With unsupervised learning, it is easy to handle real-world scenarios.

questions.question: Which regression regularization method works well in case of high dimensionality and multicollinearity among the variables in the data set?
questions.description: Correct answer: D. All of the above

questions.question: How do you select the right regression model?
options: Data exploration should be done before selecting the right model, such as identifying the appropriate model.
Cross-validation is the best way to evaluate models used for prediction.
Check whether the variables are discrete or continuous.
questions.description: Correct answer: D. All of the above

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of reinforcement learning?
questions.description: Correct answer: D. All of the above

questions.question: If a dataset does not have a target variable available, which algorithm is best suited for such a scenario?
questions.description: Correct answer: D. Both B and C

questions.question: Which is not a function of supervised learning?
questions.description: Correct answer: C. Clustering of data

questions.question: Which is not a function of unsupervised learning?
options: Finding a low-dimensional representation of data
questions.description: Correct answer: C. Regression

questions.question: How does cross-validation make sure your model is robust?
options: You train the model on a different training set and the reports are then reported in aggregate.
You train the model on a different test set and the reports are then reported in aggregate.
You train the model on a different test set as well as on the training set and reports are then reported in aggregate.
Cross-validation is not used for model robustness.
questions.description: Correct answer: B. You train the model on a different test set and the reports are then reported in aggregate.

questions.question: What is bias in machine learning?
options: Error due to the sampling of data
questions.description: Correct answer: B. Error because of wrong assumptions

questions.question: Point out the correct statement:
options: The bias-variance decomposition was originally formulated for least-squares regression.
OLS solution provides biased regression estimates in regression.
Larger bias tends to decrease variance.
questions.description: Correct answer: A. The bias-variance decomposition was originally formulated for least-squares regression.

questions.question: What is boosting in machine learning?
options: Aggregating equally the results of weak learners built independently on random samples to create a strong learner.
Combining differently the results of weak learners built sequentially on the whole dataset to create a strong learner.
Boosting is basically about combining many strong predictive models into a stronger model.
questions.description: Correct answer: B. Combining differently the results of weak learners built sequentially on the whole dataset to create a strong learner.

questions.question: Unsupervised learning can also be used to generate or create labels and classes, on top of which one can build supervised models.
questions.description: Correct answer: A. True

questions.question: Machine-learning applications are too complex to run in the cloud.
questions.description: Correct answer: B. False

questions.question: Although machine learning is useful, it can be difficult to implement. What is one common obstacle faced by businesses looking to use machine-learning tools?
options: Not enough data to perform machine-learning processes
Lack of necessary skills, including data science and Hadoop
No need for the analytics processing power in machine-learning programs
questions.description: Correct answer: B. Lack of necessary skills, including data science and Hadoop

questions.question: Which of the following is a technique frequently used in machine-learning and AI programs?
options: Classification of data into categories based on attributes
Grouping similar objects into clusters of related events
Identifying relationships between events to predict when one will follow the other
All of the above are common machine-learning techniques
questions.description: Correct answer: D. All of the above are common machine-learning techniques

questions.question: Why is it common for machine learning to be done on a Hadoop-based big-data framework?
options: Most machine-learning applications can only be run on Hadoop systems because of how they are programmed.
Machine learning requires large amounts of data and lots of processing power, so a big-data architecture is a good fit.
Because Hadoop is open source, it is a flexible platform that makes it easier for users to run machine-learning analyses.
questions.description: Correct answer: B. Machine learning requires large amounts of data and lots of processing power, so a big-data architecture is a good fit.

questions.question: What is the minimum number of features or variables required to perform clustering?
questions.description: Correct answer: B. One

questions.question: In which neural-network architecture does weight sharing occur?
questions.description: Correct answer: D. Both B and C

questions.question: Suppose that you have to minimize the cost function by changing the parameters. Which of the following techniques could be used for this?
questions.description: Correct answer: D. Any of these

questions.question: Decision trees could be used in which of the following types of problems?
questions.description: Correct answer: C. Both of the above

questions.question: The decision boundary is perpendicular to the instance-space axes for which of the following?
questions.description: Correct answer: A. Classification trees

questions.question: The test dataset used to evaluate the model is typically called a:
questions.description: Correct answer: C. Holdout dataset

questions.question: ______ learning is a framework for investigating the complexity of machine-learning problems.
questions.description: Correct answer: B. Probably Approximately Correct

questions.question: A tree-structure model could be used for:
questions.description: Correct answer: C. Classification
